behavioral_profile.one_line_description: Technical professional focused on integrating AI architectures into scalable, real-world backend infrastructure projects.

working_style.decision_making: Prioritizes functional feasibility and proven architectural performance over theoretical models.
working_style.collaboration: Prefers structured cross-functional alignment within large-scale institutional frameworks.
working_style.conflict_style: Resolves technical bottlenecks through standardization and objective, data-driven optimization.
working_style.pace: Consistent, methodically scaling systems according to predefined project roadmaps.

energizers: Transitioning ML from pilot to scale

how_to_open: Lead with specific technical hurdles in scaling systems
Acknowledge their role in bridging backend and AI
how_to_run_meeting: Focus on architecture design and capacity strengthening
Structure around concrete project milestones and roadmaps
Maintain a technical, results-oriented discussion format
how_to_email: Avoid vague or high-level strategic speculation
how_to_follow_up: Reference specific progress on deployment or integration
Ask for feedback on implementation constraints
how_to_build_trust: Deliver robust, clean, and scalable code
Show competence in multi-tool integration like YOLOv8

blind_spots: Over-emphasis on technical perfection over user-facing UI/UX
Potential tunnel vision regarding non-technical stakeholder priorities
Risk of prioritizing system robustness at the expense of agility

dos: Discuss RAG and LLM architecture integration
Focus on system scalability and data standards
Prioritize technical depth and implementation rigor
donts: Avoid abstract concepts without technical proof
Do not ignore backend architectural constraints
Do not bypass standardized communication channels

data.data_confidence_rationale: Analysis based on professional profile summary and specific participation in institutional data-standardization projects.
